title: Judge rules Trump administration violated the First Amendment in fight against ICE-tracking
summary: A federal judge ruled the Trump administration violated the First Amendment by pressuring Facebook and Apple to remove ICE-tracking apps and groups. The decision cites a 2024 Supreme Court precedent limiting government pressure on tech platforms to suppress content. The ruling grants a preliminary injunction protecting ICE-tracking apps and Facebook groups.
content:
In some Minneapolis neighborhoods, nearly every house has an anti-ICE sign. | Photo by Jack Califano / The Verge	

Jorge L. Alonso, a federal district court judge for the Northern District of Illinois, said that the Trump Administration violated the First Amendment when it pressured Facebook and Apple to remove ICE-tracking groups and apps. Judge Alonso granted the plaintiffs, Kassandra Rosado, who runs the ICE Sightings - Chicagoland Facebook group, and Kreisau Group, the developers of Eyes Up, a preliminary injunction.
Judge Alonso cited a unanimous Supreme Court decision from a 2024 case that pitted the NRA against the former superintendent of the New York Department of Financial Services, Maria Vullo. In that case, the court ruled that "[g]overnment …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 法官裁定特朗普政府在反对ICE追踪中违反了第一修正案
zh.summary: 联邦法官裁定特朗普政府通过施压Facebook和Apple删除ICE追踪应用和群组来违反第一修正案。该裁决引用2024年最高法院的先例，限制政府向科技平台施压以压制言论。该裁定授予初步禁令以保护ICE追踪应用和Facebook群组。

title: Cheap stuff that doesn’t suck, take 3
summary: The Verge staff compiled their favorite gadgets under $50 amid rising tariffs and global supply chain pressures driving up electronics costs. The curated selection emphasizes durable, practical items like power banks, key tools, and budget earbuds that deliver quality despite low prices. The roundup addresses how consumers can find reliable tech without major expenditures.
content:
From fancy OLED TVs to robot vacuums with arms, we tend to cover a lot of cool stuff here at The Verge that, unfortunately, often costs as much as a month’s rent (or more). But with the ongoing tariff situation in the US and a global memory shortage pushing up the cost of, well, everything, we’re well aware that not everyone can spend that kind of money right now.
So, as we did last year, we asked The Verge staff to share some of their favorite gadgets under $50. These are the gizmos that don’t fall apart after a few weeks, as cheap stuff often does, and quietly improve our lives. Some are practical purchases, like portable power banks and backup bulbs, while the rest are useful in other ways. After all, who doesn’t want a dirt-cheap Bluetooth speaker, a feature-packed pair of earbuds, or a fitness tracker that punches well above its weight?
If you’re curious, you can check out our favorites below.
Nite Ize DoohicKey Plus Key Tool
Nite Ize DoohicKey Plus Key Tool

Where to Buy:

 $6.99 $6.49 at Amazon
 $6.99 at Nite Ize
 $7 $6.5 at REI

This keychain thingy costs less than seven bucks, is about the size of an actual key, and clips onto your keyring with a built-in carabiner latch. It’s perfectly suited for opening boxes, popping bottlecaps, or tightening a screw or a bolt in a pinch. While it’s not as good as a dedicated tool — or even a larger multitool like a Leatherman — at any of those things, I’m also way more likely to have it on me when I need it. — Nathan Edwards, senior reviews editor
CMF Buds 2A
CMF Buds 2A

Where to Buy:

 $49 $19.99 at Amazon (black)
 $49 $19.99 at Amazon (orange)
 $49 $19.99 at Amazon (light gray)

When it comes to earbuds, my daily drivers are a pair of AirPods Pro 3. However, given how often I find myself sitting in my car due to my extracurriculars (I volunteer with search and rescue), I decided to pick up a pair of Nothing’s CMF Buds 2A as a backup for my bag. The budget earbuds are often on sale for a tenth of what the Pro 3 cost,

zh.title: 便宜又好用的东西，第3期
zh.summary: 在关税上升和全球供应链压力推高电子成本之际，《The Verge》员工编制了他们最喜爱的50美元以下的小工具。精选强调耐用实用的物品，如充电宝、钥匙工具和经济型耳塞，价格低廉但质量可靠。该评测展示消费者如何在花费较少的情况下找到优质科技产品。

title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
summary: Despite being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic continues high-level discussions with Trump administration officials. The ongoing engagement suggests potential thawing of tensions between the AI company and the administration. The development reveals complex dynamics in government-AI industry relations.
content: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

zh.title: Anthropic与特朗普政府的关系似乎在解冻
zh.summary: 尽管被五角大楼指定为供应链风险，Anthropic继续与特朗普政府官员进行高层次讨论。持续的接触表明这家AI公司与政府之间的紧张关系可能在缓解。该进展揭示了政府与AI产业关系中的复杂动态。

title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
summary: New data from Appfigures shows a significant surge in app launches on the App Store in 2026, potentially reversing years of mobile software stagnation. AI tools appear to be driving this boom by enabling developers to create and deploy applications more efficiently. The trend signals renewed developer interest in the mobile platform.
content: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

zh.title: App Store再次繁荣，AI可能是推手
zh.summary: 来自Appfigures的新数据显示，2026年App Store的应用发布数量大幅增长，可能扭转多年的移动软件停滞局面。人工智能工具似乎通过使开发者能够更高效地创建和部署应用来推动这波繁荣。这一趋势表明开发者对移动平台的兴趣正在重新增长。

title: Dyson’s handheld fan is more powerful and louder than I expected
summary: Dyson's HushJet Mini Cool handheld fan at $99.99 delivers powerful performance in a compact 208-gram form factor with five speeds plus boost mode. Despite its "Hush" branding, the fan is notably loud and achieves only 62 minutes of runtime at high speed rather than the claimed six-hour battery life at lowest settings. Its enclosed cylindrical design prioritizes portability and safety compared to competitors with exposed blades.
content:
Two years ago I attended a picturesque outdoor wedding in August where the hot and humid weather had guests occasionally ducking into their cars to enjoy a blast of AC. Dyson’s new $99.99 handheld fan would have provided some much-needed relief then, although I’m not sure I would have been comfortable powering it up during the service or reception. The HushJet Mini Cool is as slim and sleek as Dyson’s recent thin vacuum and hair dryer, but it’s not quiet enough to earn the name “Hush.”
That’s unfortunate because the HushJet Mini Cool is yet another product Dyson has redesigned to look and perform better than what’s already on the market. All of its components — including the motor, 5,000mAh rechargeable battery, and spinning blades — are safely contained inside a cylindrical body 38mm in diameter that’s easy to slip into a pocket. Many handheld fans use flexible or folding blades that are left exposed and not ideal for kids, or they stuff the moving parts into a bulbous section on top that limits pocketability.
Dyson HushJet Mini Cool

Where to Buy:

 $99.99 at Dyson
 $99.99 at Amazon
 $99.99 at Best Buy

At 208 grams the HushJet Mini Cool is about the same weight as an iPhone 17 Pro, so it’s not a terrible burden to schlep around when temperatures soar, and it’s incredibly easy to use. A toggle switch turns the fan on and off, while a rocker button lets you step up and down through five different fan speeds indicated by a strip of five small white LEDs. There’s also an even stronger Boost Mode that’s only activated while you’re holding down the “plus” side of that rocker button.
The only learning curve was remembering to grip the fan above the vent that wraps around its base so as not to block the airflow. Some added texture on the outside of the HushJet Mini Cool to naturally guide where your hand should grip it would be welcome.
Dyson claims up to six hours of battery life at the fan’s lowest setting, but at its highest speed (not Boost Mode) it died after 62 min

zh.title: 戴森手持风扇比预期更强大、更大声
zh.summary: 戴森HushJet Mini Cool手持风扇定价99.99美元，以208克的轻巧外形提供强大性能，具有五档速度加强力模式。尽管产品名称为"静音"，但该风扇的噪音明显偏大，高速模式下仅能运行62分钟，远低于最低速设置下六小时的宣传续航。相比竞争对手暴露的叶片设计，其密闭圆柱形设计更优先考虑便携性和安全性。

title: There’s nothing like an RPG over vacation
summary: Players seeking chill, immersive games for vacation time are discovering People of Note, a music-focused RPG from Annapurna Interactive and Iridium Studios. The game follows Cadence, an aspiring pop singer competing against a rival boy band, offering the perfect escapist experience for a week-long getaway.
content:
With a vacation comes a big choice: What game should I focus on during the trip? I thought about grinding out the harder levels of Super Meat Boy 3D, but I was looking for something more chill. I could have dabbled more with Slay the Spire II, but I already know that's a game I'll be playing for a long time. I wanted something that I could really get lost in and finish in a little over a week. People of Note, a new music-focused RPG from Annapurna Interactive and Iridium Studios, turned out to be exactly what I needed.
In the game, you play as aspiring pop singer Cadence. What starts as a journey to outperform a popular boy band turns into  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 假期中没有什么比得上RPG游戏
zh.summary: 寻求假期休闲沉浸式游戏的玩家发现了《Note的人们》,这是Annapurna Interactive和Iridium Studios推出的以音乐为主题的RPG游戏。游戏跟随有志成为流行歌手的Cadence,与竞争对手男孩乐队较量,为期一周的假期提供了完美的逃避体验。

title: The AI apps are coming for your PC
summary: AI applications are rapidly expanding into personal computing as a significant new software category. The Verge examines this emerging trend of AI tools designed for consumer PCs alongside other notable developments in technology and culture.
content:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 124, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, send me your Coachella fits, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
This week, I've been reading about restaurant bread and GLP-1s  and Lenny Rachitsky and Artemis II fashion, watching the new boy band doc because I will always watch a boy band doc, also watching every clip I can find from Justin Bieber's Coachella set, filling the Schitt's Creek-shaped hole in my heart with Big Mistakes, getting increasingly excited about The Mandalorian and Grogu, and watering my new lawn so it …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: AI应用程序正在进入你的个人电脑
zh.summary: AI应用程序作为一个重要的新软件类别,正在迅速扩展到个人计算领域。The Verge审视了这一新兴趋势,即为消费者PC设计的AI工具,以及科技和文化的其他重要发展。

title: Great white sharks are overheating
summary: Great white sharks face severe physiological stress as ocean temperatures rise due to climate change. Scientists warn these apex predators are among the most vulnerable marine species to rapid warming in their habitats.
content: The sharks might also be the most physiologically vulnerable to warming waters.

zh.title: 大白鲨正在过热
zh.summary: 由于气候变化导致海洋温度上升,大白鲨面临严重的生理压力。科学家警告称,这些顶级掠食者是对其栖息地快速升温最为脆弱的海洋物种之一。

title: Once close enough for an acquisition, Stripe and Airwallex are now going after each other
summary: Stripe and Airwallex, once serving different geographies and customer segments, are now directly competing as their business territories overlap. This shift signals intensifying competition in the global fintech payments market.
content: For most of its life, Airwallex and Stripe have mostly operated in different geographies, selling to different buyers. That's changing.

zh.title: 曾接近被对方收购，Stripe和Airwallex现在却在竞争
zh.summary: Stripe和Airwallex曾经服务于不同的地区和客户群体，现在随着业务领地重叠，两者正在直接竞争。这一转变表明全球金融科技支付市场竞争日益加剧。
